text: View on gun control, abortion, and budget

i don't care for their way of saying that the republic party always takes from the poor and gives to the rich//becasue the rich pay more in taxes, when there is a tax break they would get more back in taxes//i wish they wouln't attach other parties as noted previously also for the republican party

I like how most have differing viewpoints.
pro-gun, pro-life
Pushing socialism. Too many handout programs instead of handup! programs teaching skills.
Trying to get things done instead of kicking the can down the road.
Sometimes they spend too much unnecessarily.
More for the middle class. More social welfare programs. More fair taxing.
For big business. Too much for defense spending.

Sometimes I feel like they use social issues to get elected and don't follow through. In general I don't feel they make choices as well when it comes to how to spend limited funds. They can take credit for a lot of the National debt since the 1950's
I think they pay more attention to social issues -- elderly, homeless, handicapped, workers.

Their emphasis is Business and tax loopholes for business and the wealthy. Not enough concern with social issues particularly health care. They back special interest groups too much, such as the doctors and hospitals. They back the wealthy and influential sides of issues.
They're concerned about unemployment. They seem to care a great deal about the middle class -- encouraging professionals.
Shifting too far left of center
Total loss of a conservative identity with a cult of Trump

If Republicans or Democrats win, Congress does the approving anyway. They're more honest. If Reagan has something important to the country that they need to know, he'll tell you on T.V. You get a fair shake from the Democrats.
MORE CONSERVATIVE WAYS , MORE BUSINESS, /AE/NO
